activities.title: Trail Trust Traverse
activities.description: Students work in small groups to navigate a low-ropes style course using only verbal communication, emphasizing trust and collaboration.

activities.title: Pioneer Problem-Solving Challenge
activities.description: Teams rotate through quick problem-solving stations that require creativity, communication, and cooperative decision-making.

activities.title: River Crossing Initiative Game
activities.description: Using limited supplies, groups must get their entire team across an imaginary river without touching the ground.

activities.title: Summit Leadership Circle
activities.description: Facilitated discussion and challenge activities that help students explore different leadership styles and roles.

activities.title: Backcountry Scenario Simulation
activities.description: Students rotate through realistic outdoor scenarios where they must delegate tasks and make group decisions under time pressure.

activities.title: Forest Ecology Field Walk
activities.description: Guided hike focusing on forest layers, habitats, and local plant and animal adaptations with hands-on data collection.

activities.title: Forest Plot Study
activities.description: Students collect data in sample plots to investigate biodiversity, tree health, and evidence of human impact.

activities.title: Stream Study & Macroinvertebrates
activities.description: Hands-on stream study where students test water quality and identify aquatic macroinvertebrates as bioindicators.

activities.title: Watershed Model Lab
activities.description: Indoor activity using a watershed model to explore runoff, pollution, and conservation strategies.

activities.title: Backcountry Survival Basics
activities.description: Overview of the rule of threes, shelter-building demos, and safe fire-building with instructor supervision.

activities.title: Emergency Shelter Building
activities.description: Teams design and build simple emergency shelters using natural and provided materials.

activities.title: Compass & Map Navigation
activities.description: Students learn to use compasses, read contour maps, and navigate a short orienteering course.

activities.title: Intro to GPS Orienteering
activities.description: Geocaching-style navigation where teams locate hidden waypoints using handheld GPS units.

activities.title: Solar Energy Field Lab
activities.description: Hands-on experiments with portable solar panels to explore energy transfer and efficiency.

activities.title: Intro to Aerial Mapping with Drones
activities.description: Demonstration-based activity where students examine aerial images and learn about mapping ecosystems.

activities.title: Inclusive Field Games
activities.description: Low-competition, high-fun activities designed to build connection and confidence for all students.

activities.title: Trail Stewardship Mini-Project
activities.description: Students practice servant leadership through a short habitat restoration or trail maintenance project.

activities.title: Guided Night Hike
activities.description: Sensory-focused night hike exploring nocturnal adaptations, sky observation, and quiet reflection.

addon_options.name: Rock Climbing
addon_options.description: Guided top-rope rock climbing session with professional instructors and all necessary safety gear.

addon_options.name: Night Hike
addon_options.description: After-dark guided hike focusing on nocturnal wildlife, senses, and astronomy.

addon_options.name: Campfire Program
addon_options.description: Evening campfire with songs, stories, and s’mores led by staff.

addon_options.name: Premium Hot Lunch Upgrade
addon_options.description: Upgrade to a hot entree with salad bar and dessert.

addon_options.name: Round-Trip Bus Transportation
addon_options.description: Chartered bus transportation between your school and the outdoor center.

gear_rental_options.name: No rental
gear_rental_options.description: Students and staff bring all of their own gear. No rental charges apply.

gear_rental_options.name: Basic Daypack & Rain Poncho
gear_rental_options.description: Includes a lightweight daypack and disposable rain poncho for each student.

gear_rental_options.name: Overnight Essentials Bundle
gear_rental_options.description: Sleeping bag, sleeping pad, and headlamp for overnight campers.

gear_rental_options.name: Cold Weather Clothing Kit
gear_rental_options.description: Insulated jacket, snow pants, and waterproof gloves for winter programs.

meal_plans.name: Budget Box Lunch
meal_plans.description: Simple sandwich, fruit, snack, and bottled water. Nut-free options available.

meal_plans.name: Standard Box Lunch
meal_plans.description: Deli sandwich or vegetarian wrap, fruit, cookie, and drink.

meal_plans.name: Hot Cafeteria Lunch
meal_plans.description: Hot entree, side dish, salad bar, dessert, and drink.

meal_plans.name: Standard Overnight Meal Plan
meal_plans.description: Includes dinner, breakfast, and a standard box lunch each full day.

meal_plans.name: Premium Overnight Meal Plan
meal_plans.description: Expanded hot meal options, salad bar, and evening snack.

meal_plans.name: Snack-Only Add-On
meal_plans.description: Morning and afternoon snack breaks with fruit and granola bars.

meal_plans.name: No Meals Included
meal_plans.description: School or families provide all meals and snacks.

meal_plans.name: Hearty Day-Trip Lunch
meal_plans.description: Hot entree or large sandwich, fruit, veggie sticks, and dessert.

meal_plans.name: Vegetarian/Vegan Lunch Option
meal_plans.description: Plant-based wrap or salad bowl, fruit, and dairy-free dessert.

meal_plans.name: Winter Warm-Up Meal Plan
meal_plans.description: Soup or chili bar for lunch plus hot cocoa break for winter day trips.

resources.title: Forest Layers & Habitats (Grade 5)
resources.description: Lesson plan exploring canopy, understory, shrub, and forest floor layers with an outdoor observation activity.

resources.title: Decomposers in the Forest (Grade 5)
resources.description: Hands-on investigation of decomposers, nutrient cycles, and their role in forest ecosystems.

resources.title: Forest Food Web Simulation
resources.description: Interactive role-play where students build and test a forest food web model.

resources.title: Elementary Day-Trip Packing Checklist
resources.description: Printable checklist for families with recommended clothing, water, and optional items for elementary day trips.

resources.title: Overnight Outdoor Program Packing Checklist
resources.description: Detailed checklist for 2–3 night outdoor programs, including weather-dependent items.

resources.title: Team-Building Warm-Up Games
resources.description: Collection of quick icebreakers and energizers suitable for outdoor use.

resources.title: Student Leadership Reflection Journal
resources.description: Printable journal pages with prompts for leadership-focused trips.

resources.title: Water Quality Field Lab Sheet
resources.description: Data collection sheet for testing pH, turbidity, and dissolved oxygen.

resources.title: Parent Information Letter Template
resources.description: Editable letter template to communicate trip details, safety, and packing lists to families.

resources.title: Outdoor Program Safety Overview Slides
resources.description: Short slide deck to present safety expectations to students before the trip.

resources.title: Grade 9 Leadership Day Sample Schedule
resources.description: Example itinerary with three team-building and leadership blocks for Grade 9.

resources.title: Rock Cycle in the Field
resources.description: Lesson plan connecting rock samples on site to the stages of the rock cycle.

resources.title: Forest Plot Data Sheet
resources.description: Printable sheet for recording tree species, DBH, and ground cover.

resources.title: Trip Budget Planning Worksheet
resources.description: Spreadsheet-based tool for estimating total trip costs by category.

resources.title: Student Emergency Contact Card Template
resources.description: Small card format for collecting key medical and emergency contact information.

safety_sections.title: Emergency Procedures
safety_sections.contentHtml:
Our staff are trained to respond to a wide range of emergencies including severe weather, injury, illness, and missing persons. Each program begins with a safety briefing for teachers and students.

  • On-site first aid stations at all centers
  • Two-way radios and cell coverage on primary routes
  • Pre-established evacuation and shelter-in-place protocols

For families, we provide a sample letter you can customize with trip-specific details:

Download Sample Parent Information Letter

safety_sections.title: Supervision & Staffing Policies
safety_sections.contentHtml: All overnight programs maintain supervision ratios that meet or exceed state guidelines. Certified instructors lead high-adventure activities, and school staff remain responsible for in-cabin supervision at night.

safety_sections.title: Medical & Medication Policies
safety_sections.contentHtml: We work with schools to coordinate medication administration, allergy management, and emergency medical response. At least one staff member with advanced first aid training is on duty at all times.

safety_sections.title: Risk Management Overview
safety_sections.contentHtml: Our risk management plan includes annual course inspections, activity-specific operating procedures, and ongoing staff training and evaluation.

safety_sections.title: Communication with Families
safety_sections.contentHtml: We encourage schools to communicate clearly with families about program goals, safety measures, and expectations. Sample emails and letters are available in our Teacher Resources library.

programs.title: Grade 4 Forest Discovery Day
programs.shortDescription: A standards-aligned day trip focused on forest ecology and team-building for Grade 4–5 classes.
programs.fullDescription: This immersive day program introduces students to local forest ecosystems through guided hikes, hands-on investigations, and cooperative games. Designed specifically for Grade 4 science standards, classes explore forest layers, food webs, and human impact while practicing communication and collaboration.
learningOutcomes: Identify and describe the main layers of a temperate forest ecosystem.
Collect and interpret simple field data about local plants and animals.
Practice age-appropriate collaboration and communication skills.
Develop a sense of curiosity and stewardship for local natural areas.
highLevelItinerary: Morning: Arrival, welcome circle, and safety orientation.
Late Morning: Guided forest hike focused on forest layers and habitats.
Lunch: Picnic at the pavilion (school-provided or boxed lunches).
Early Afternoon: Hands-on forest investigation stations and data collection.
Late Afternoon: Cooperative team-building games and closing reflection.

programs.title: Grade 5 Stream & Watershed Study Day
programs.shortDescription: Hands-on stream investigation and watershed model lab for upper elementary students.
programs.fullDescription: Students spend the day exploring how streams shape the land and support aquatic life. In small groups they test water quality, collect macroinvertebrates, and use a watershed model to see how pollution travels. The program reinforces Grade 5 earth and life science standards while building observation and teamwork skills.
learningOutcomes: Explain how watersheds connect land and water systems.
Use field tools to measure basic indicators of water quality.
Identify common aquatic macroinvertebrates as bioindicators.
Discuss human actions that affect freshwater systems and propose solutions.
highLevelItinerary: Morning: Orientation and introduction to watersheds.
Late Morning: Stream study rotation with sampling and water testing.
Lunch: Picnic lunch near the stream.
Early Afternoon: Indoor watershed model demonstration and discussion.
Late Afternoon: Small-group reflection and bus departure.

programs.title: Elementary Spring Explorer Overnight (1 Night)
programs.shortDescription: A first-time overnight experience for Grades 4–6 with ecology, navigation, and campfire program.
programs.fullDescription: This one-night spring program is ideal for classes ready to try an overnight outdoor experience. Students rotate through forest ecology, navigation, and team-building sessions during the day and enjoy a structured campfire program in the evening. Teachers can choose to emphasize science, social-emotional learning, or a mix of both.
includedActivitiesSummary: Evening campfire with songs and stories
learningOutcomes: Build confidence by successfully participating in an overnight group experience.
Describe key features of local forest ecosystems.
Use a compass and map to navigate a short orienteering course.
Strengthen classroom community through shared challenges and reflection.
highLevelItinerary: Day 1 Morning: Arrival, orientation, and forest ecology field walk.
Day 1 Afternoon: Team-building initiatives and navigation course.
Evening: Dinner in the dining hall followed by campfire program.
Day 2 Morning: Choice activity block and closing reflection circle.
Day 2 Midday: Lunch and departure.

programs.title: Winter STEM Expedition (2-Night)
programs.shortDescription: A two-night winter program for Grades 7–8 combining ecology, STEM investigations, and adventure.
programs.fullDescription: Designed for middle school science teams, this two-night winter expedition blends outdoor fieldwork with indoor STEM investigations. Students explore snow and ice as materials, investigate winter wildlife adaptations, and complete a design challenge related to cold-weather shelters or energy use. Optional high-adventure add-ons include rock climbing and a guided night hike.
learningOutcomes: Apply the engineering design process to a real-world cold-weather challenge.
Analyze how local wildlife survives and thrives in winter conditions.
Develop collaboration and problem-solving skills in small teams.
Strengthen resilience and a growth mindset through outdoor challenges.
highLevelItinerary: Day 1: Arrival, winter ecology hike, team-building initiatives, and evening group games.
Day 2 Morning: STEM design challenge lab focused on energy transfer and insulation.
Day 2 Afternoon: Choice blocks including low ropes or snow-based field activities.
Evening: Optional night hike add-on or indoor reflection circles.
Day 3: Closing activity rotations, student presentations, and departure.

programs.title: Snow & Stars Leadership Retreat (2-Night)
programs.shortDescription: A winter leadership-focused overnight for Grade 7–8 student councils and leadership groups.
programs.fullDescription: This two-night retreat helps student leaders practice communication, decision-making, and inclusion in a winter outdoor setting. Facilitated leadership workshops are balanced with time outdoors exploring the winter landscape. Evening options include a reflective night hike and a campfire circle.
learningOutcomes: Recognize and practice multiple leadership styles in group settings.
Improve communication and conflict-resolution skills.
Develop concrete action steps to bring leadership lessons back to school.
Build trust and connection among student leaders from the same school.
highLevelItinerary: Day 1: Arrival, leadership icebreakers, and team-building initiatives.
Day 2 Morning: Leadership style workshop and scenario simulations.
Day 2 Afternoon: Outdoor winter hike or snowshoe route (conditions permitting).
Evening: Optional night hike add-on and reflection circle.
Day 3: Action planning session, closing ceremony, and departure.

programs.title: Summer Adventure Camp (2-Night)
programs.shortDescription: A two-night summer adventure for Grades 6–8 featuring rock climbing, team-building, and a night hike.
programs.fullDescription: This popular two-night summer program focuses on adventure, collaboration, and personal growth for middle school groups. Students rotate through rock climbing, navigation, and team-building sessions during the day and participate in a guided night hike and campfire program in the evenings. The program is ideal for welcoming new grade cohorts or launching leadership initiatives.
includedActivitiesSummary: Campfire program with songs and skits
learningOutcomes: Build confidence by attempting appropriately challenging adventure activities.
Develop collaboration, trust, and problem-solving skills.
Practice basic navigation and risk management in outdoor settings.
Strengthen group identity and sense of belonging before the school year.
highLevelItinerary: Day 1: Arrival, orientation, team-building rotations, and evening campfire.
Day 2 Morning: Rock climbing sessions with professional instructors.
Day 2 Afternoon: Navigation challenge and choice activity block.
Evening: Guided night hike add-on focusing on nocturnal ecology.
Day 3: Closing reflection, group challenge, and departure.

programs.title: Middle School Team-Building & Leadership Day
programs.shortDescription: A one-day program for Grades 7–9 that focuses on teamwork, communication, and leadership.
programs.fullDescription: Ideal for beginning-of-year community building or leadership cohorts, this program uses progressive initiatives and reflection to help students practice effective teamwork and leadership. Activities are tailored to the group’s goals, from improving homeroom culture to preparing a student council team.
learningOutcomes: Demonstrate effective communication and active listening in group tasks.
Recognize the roles of different leadership styles within a team.
Identify strategies to foster an inclusive and supportive classroom community.
highLevelItinerary: Morning: Arrival, welcome, and warm-up initiatives.
Late Morning: Low ropes and leadership challenge rotations.
Afternoon: Large-group games, reflection, and goal-setting.
Late Afternoon: Closing circle and departure.

programs.title: High School Leadership Summit (2-Night)
programs.shortDescription: A leadership-intensive retreat for Grades 9–12 student leaders and peer mentors.
programs.fullDescription: This two-night summit brings high school leaders together for intensive workshops, outdoor challenges, and facilitated reflection. Schools often send student council members, club officers, or peer mentors to clarify their goals and build a shared vision for the year.
learningOutcomes: Clarify personal leadership strengths and growth areas.
Practice facilitation and peer-mentoring techniques.
Develop a concrete action plan to improve school culture.
Strengthen trust and collaboration among student leaders.
highLevelItinerary: Day 1: Arrival, leadership warm-ups, and team initiatives.
Day 2: Workshops, outdoor challenges, and evening campfire.
Day 3: Action planning, peer feedback, and closing ceremony.

programs.title: Grade 4–5 Navigation Quest Day
programs.shortDescription: A day trip for Grades 4–5 introducing map skills, compasses, and cooperative problem-solving.
programs.fullDescription: Students learn basic navigation skills through hands-on stations and a navigation quest around the outdoor center. The program integrates math, geography, and teamwork as small groups use maps and compasses to locate checkpoints and solve simple challenges.
learningOutcomes: Use a compass to follow basic bearings.
Interpret simple maps and symbols to navigate a course.
Collaborate with peers to solve location-based challenges.
Build confidence in outdoor exploration in a structured setting.
highLevelItinerary: Morning: Arrival, safety briefing, and compass introduction.
Late Morning: Map-reading lesson and skill practice stations.
Afternoon: Navigation quest in teams around the outdoor center.
Late Afternoon: Reflection, celebration, and departure.

programs.title: Spring Ecology & Stewardship Camp (3-Night)
programs.shortDescription: A three-night intensive ecology program for Grades 6–8 with a focus on stewardship projects.
programs.fullDescription: Over three nights, students dive deep into forest and water ecology, data collection, and service projects that support the health of the outdoor center. The program is well suited for science academies or grade-level teams that want extended field time and a culminating stewardship experience.
learningOutcomes: Apply scientific practices to collect and analyze field data over multiple days.
Explain interactions among forest and aquatic ecosystems.
Participate in a service project that benefits a local natural area.
Reflect on personal and collective responsibility for environmental stewardship.
highLevelItinerary: Day 1: Arrival, orientation, and introductory ecology hike.
Day 2: Forest plot studies and data collection rotations.
Day 3: Stream investigations and stewardship service project.
Evenings: Night hike or campfire, journaling, and reflection circles.
Day 4: Data synthesis, presentations, and departure.

programs.title: High School Outdoor STEM Discovery Day
programs.shortDescription: A STEM-focused day trip for Grades 9–12 featuring solar energy and drone mapping demos.
programs.fullDescription: In this STEM-centered day program, high school students explore renewable energy and mapping technologies in an outdoor setting. They rotate through a solar energy lab, a demonstration of aerial mapping with drones, and an applied data-collection activity tailored to their science or engineering coursework.
includedActivitiesSummary: Intro to Aerial Mapping with Drones
learningOutcomes: Analyze how solar energy can be captured and used in field settings.
Describe applications of aerial mapping and remote sensing for environmental monitoring.
Connect outdoor STEM experiences to classroom physics or engineering concepts.
highLevelItinerary: Morning: Arrival and solar energy field lab.
Midday: Lunch and campus exploration.
Afternoon: Drone mapping demonstration and data analysis session.
Late Afternoon: Reflection and departure.

programs.title: Custom Curriculum-Aligned Day Program
programs.shortDescription: A flexible day trip tailored to your grade level, subject focus, and time frame.
programs.fullDescription: Work with our program team to design a custom day trip that aligns with your curriculum and student needs. Options include forest or water ecology investigations, team-building, leadership development, navigation skills, and STEM-focused activities. Ideal for multi-grade outings or interdisciplinary projects.
includedActivitiesSummary: Choice of two to three curriculum-aligned activity blocks
Flexible mix of ecology, STEM, and team-building sessions
Closing reflection tailored to your learning goals
learningOutcomes: Reinforce key curriculum standards through hands-on field experiences.
Customize social-emotional and leadership outcomes for your group.
Engage students in inquiry-based learning connected to their community.
highLevelItinerary: Morning: Arrival, welcome, and first choice activity block.
Midday: Second activity block and lunch.
Afternoon: Third activity block and closing reflection.
Late Afternoon: Departure.

safety_documents.description: A sample parent letter outlining emergency procedures, communication plans, and key safety practices for overnight and day programs.

safety_documents.title: Participant Medical & Waiver Form
safety_documents.description: Standard form for collecting medical information, emergency contacts, and consent for participation.

safety_documents.title: Overnight Supervision & Cabin Checklist
safety_documents.description: Checklist for teachers and chaperones outlining expectations for nighttime supervision, cabin checks, and student safety.
